methodology: framework-neutral agent relationship architecture by default; package metadata has no dependency on any one multi-agent runtime
principle: Separate agent architecture from workflow orchestration. Use profile-based agents as graph nodes and explicit edges for routing, handoffs, gates, fan-out/fan-in, checkpoints, and terminal boundaries.

patterns.name: Coordinator + Specialists
patterns.use_when: Most nontrivial teams need a small entry/coordinator plus specialist producers and an independent reviewer.

patterns.name: Supervisor / Worker
patterns.use_when: One role should route work, manage state, and synthesize specialist outputs.

patterns.name: Router + Specialist Pools
patterns.use_when: Inputs fall into distinct task classes and only some specialists should run per task.
patterns.agent_count_fit: 5-6, or fewer when classes are simple

patterns.name: Handoff Chain
patterns.use_when: Ownership should move from one accountable role to another with explicit inputs, outputs, and gates.

patterns.name: Evidence Ledger / Blackboard
patterns.use_when: Multiple roles consume shared evidence, claims, artifacts, or state that must stay versioned.

patterns.name: Reviewer-Gated
patterns.use_when: Producer or executor output must be independently accepted before delivery or side effects.

patterns.name: Team ReAct Loop
patterns.use_when: Reason / act / observe behavior crosses agent boundaries instead of staying inside one agent.
patterns.agent_count_fit: 5-6 only when actions and observations have separate accountability

patterns.name: Hybrid Routing
patterns.use_when: Simple tasks should stay single-agent or two-role, while complex or risky tasks activate the full team.
patterns.agent_count_fit: 2-4 for simple path with consolidation rationale, 5-6 for full path

selection_rules: Default to framework-neutral agent architecture relationship graph construction for design/package outputs unless the user requests another architecture.
Prefer 5-6 top-level agents for nontrivial teams.
Do not split a workflow step into an agent merely because it is a step.
Use one agent for one durable accountability boundary, not one action.
Keep ReAct as an agent-internal control pattern unless separate roles own reasoning, acting, and observing.
Escalate beyond 6 only for strong independence, isolation, domain, credential, or governance requirements.
